g.text: Leaf Page vs Internal Page Structure
No right_child field (leaf has no children)
Cell Pointer Array (2 bytes each, sorted by rowid)
Array grows DOWNWARD as cells are added
Cell Pointer Array (2 bytes each, sorted by separator key)
Internal cells carry ONLY separator keys + child pointers
No row data means higher fanout (more keys per page)
record_header | serial types for each column
~50-200 bytes per cell (depends on row width)
No payload_size, no record, no row data
Leaf: 70 rows/page | tree height for 1M rows = 5
3 page reads to find any row among 39 million
Each page read = one buffer pool fetch (~10ns hit)
internal pages route searches to correct leaf
cell format determines fanout and tree height
